clause_anchor.span: After the relationship ends, Vendor will remove hosted account content within 90 days and will not be required to provide written completion evidence.

deviations.original_text: Upon termination or expiration of the services, or earlier upon Customer's written request, Vendor will return or delete Personal Data at Customer's direction within 30 days and certify completion in writing.
deviations.mutated_text: After the relationship ends, Vendor will remove hosted account content within 90 days and will not be required to provide written completion evidence.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor may move hosted records among facilities and affiliates in the United States, Canada, Ireland, India, and Singapore without standard contractual clauses or transfer impact documentation.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will not transfer Personal Data to another country unless a lawful transfer mechanism is in place, including standard contractual clauses or another legally recognized safeguard where required.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor may move hosted records among facilities and affiliates in the United States, Canada, Ireland, India, and Singapore without standard contractual clauses or transfer impact documentation.

clause_anchor.span: For matters arising under this addendum, total recovery against Vendor will not exceed fees paid for the affected service during the three months before the event, and consequential loss is unavailable.

deviations.original_text: Claims arising from Vendor's breach of this DPA are subject to the liability cap and exclusions stated in the master services agreement and are not subject to any lower cap, special exclusion, or separate damages waiver for data protection obligations.
deviations.mutated_text: For matters arising under this addendum, total recovery against Vendor will not exceed fees paid for the affected service during the three months before the event, and consequential loss is unavailable.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor will maintain TLS in transit and an annual access review, and may replace other controls on 30 days' notice if monthly production uptime remains at 99.5%.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards for hosted Personal Data, including encryption at rest and in transit, role-based access controls, multi-factor authentication for administrative access, security logging, vulnerability management, backup controls, and incident response procedures.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor will maintain TLS in transit and an annual access review, and may replace other controls on 30 days' notice if monthly production uptime remains at 99.5%.

distractors.span: Vendor will provide an annual SOC 2 Type II report, responses to security and privacy questionnaires within 10 business days, and written control mappings needed to demonstrate compliance with this DPA.
distractors.why_benign: The audit canary says an annual SOC 2 Type II report may satisfy routine assurance if follow-up rights remain available.

distractors.span: For up to 30 days after termination, Vendor will provide export files, transfer coordination, and deletion support for secure transition, return, or deletion.
distractors.why_benign: The transition canary says a 30-day termination assistance period is acceptable.

distractors.span: If this DPA conflicts with the master services agreement regarding Personal Data, this DPA controls for that Processing.
distractors.why_benign: A DPA precedence clause for personal-data processing is customary and client-protective.

missing_info.topic: Vendor obligation to maintain records of processing activities
missing_info.context_anchor: Customer must demonstrate processor accountability to its own regulators and relies on Vendor to maintain records of processing activities for the services.
